description: Multi-language automated exam generation and grading system

features.name: Authentication & Session Management API
features.description: Provides user registration, login, role verification, and secure JWT session management.

features.name: Database Migrations Engine
features.description: Uses Alembic migrations to automatically apply and rollback database schema changes.
features.evidence: Alembic configured in backend/alembic (env.py reads DATABASE_URL from settings, engine built in code so no creds in alembic.ini). 2 migrations: 3a99aac81951 (baseline 7 tables) + 69936c2cdac2 (M2: blueprints, import_batches, content_hash/source_question_id/import_batch_id). Verified MANUALLY: `alembic upgrade head` runs clean on empty PostgreSQL 17 (both revisions applied; Migration 2's ALTER ADD CONSTRAINT is PG-only, fails on SQLite by design). No automated pytest coverage — tests use create_all() on SQLite in-memory. Task 0c, PR #16 (0c265c9).

features.name: Exam & Question Bank CRUD API
features.description: Enables teachers to manage exams, view question banks, and approve imported questions.

features.name: Candidate Exam Room Front-end
features.description: Provides webcam proctoring hooks, tab visibility checking, and compressed Opus/WebM audio recording.

features.name: Gemini AI Grading Service
features.description: Connects to Gemini API to grade writing essays and speaking audio recordings based on rubrics.
features.evidence: backend/app/services/ai_grading.py — grade_writing()/grade_speaking() with safe mock fallback; verified by tests/test_specs_grading.py::test_SPEC_GRADE_004_ai_grading_degrades_safely (SPEC-GRADE-004 active).

features.name: Background Task Grading Queue
features.description: Celery workers consume submitted candidate exams from Redis queue and trigger asynchronous grading.
features.evidence: app/core/celery.py configured; app/workers/tasks.py defines grade_submission_task — NOT yet wired to the submit API (grading runs synchronously; SPEC-GRADE-003 planned, no test yet).

features.name: English Exam Ingestion Engine
features.description: Parses Word/Excel/MP3 data, extracts sections, maps audio files, and runs UTF-8 formatting validators.
features.evidence: backend/app/services/parser.py — import_file(db, filepath, audio_dir=None) parses .docx [Group]/[Question] blocks (python-docx), validation gate (missing options/answer, invalid part, missing Listening audio file), atomic save via ImportBatch, idempotent dedup via content_hash on bank (exam_id IS NULL), items stored status='draft'. Verified by tests/test_specs_parser.py: SPEC-PARSE-001/002/003/004 active (no incomplete questions; Listening audio existence per convention {SetID}_P{Part}_{NN}.mp3; idempotent reimport; all-or-nothing + JSON error report). A3 (d2388e8, SPEC-PARSE-005 active): parse_answer_key(filepath) reads answer keys from separate .xlsx (openpyxl, scans 'Câu'/'Đáp án' header, merges 5 column-blocks) — VERIFIED on REAL partner file Key LT2601.xlsx (100 answers, matches actual data). IMPORTANT: current import_file parses a SYNTHETIC .docx format ([Group]/[Question], inline answers) that does NOT match real partner docs (real = Word tables + images, answers in separate .xlsx). Real parser track STEP ① DONE — parse_listening_docx(filepath)->{set_id, items} (SPEC-PARSE-006 active); STEP ② DONE — import_listening_set(db, docx_path, key_path) merges answer keys into questions by Mã đề+number, validates items, registers ImportBatch, and commits all questions/groups to the DB question bank with status='draft' (SPEC-PARSE-007 active). Fixed question-level hash collision in Part 1 and Part 2 (where identical content and empty options caused duplicate elimination) by incorporating set_id and question number in calculate_question_hash and set_id in calculate_group_hash, while preserving backward compatibility. Verified on real file pair LT2601.docx + Key LT2601.xlsx to produce 100/100 unique question hashes and 23/23 group hashes with 0 collisions. STEP ③ DONE — convert_doc_to_docx(filepath, out_dir=None) converts legacy .doc -> .docx via headless LibreOffice (soffice --headless --convert-to docx), with .docx passthrough, result caching, and clear FileNotFoundError when soffice missing (SPEC-PARSE-008 active). CI tests are MOCK-ONLY (passthrough + missing-tool + cache via tmp_path); REAL .doc conversion VERIFIED on machine (15/06, LibreOffice 26.2 installed): convert_doc_to_docx round-trips a real binary .doc -> .docx preserving 15 tables, python-docx reads output (soffice headless via the function's Windows fallback path). Actual partner RT*.doc will be exercised when building the Reading parser. STEP ④ DONE — parse_reading_docx(filepath)->{set_id, items} parses real Reading RT*.docx (SPEC-PARSE-009 active): P5 standalone (Q1-30), P6 4-blank passages (Q31-46), P7 single/double/triple passages with images (Q47-100); robust option extraction handling inline-A, all-options-inline (re.DOTALL + regex-split on (A-D) markers), sandwich quotation paragraphs, and 4x2 option tables. VERIFIED on REAL 'CDR TOEIC - RT2605.docx': 100 questions, 0 incomplete (every question exactly 4 options A-D). Two fixture-invisible bugs caught at Claude review and fixed: infinite loop (inner loop never advanced j) and option loss (30/70 grouped questions). STEP ⑤ DONE — import_exam_set(db, docx_path, key_path, exam_type, audio_dir=None) generalizes merge+import for BOTH Listening and Reading (dispatch parser by exam_type, LT|RT set_id); import_listening_set/import_reading_set are thin wrappers (SPEC-PARSE-010 active). VERIFIED on real data (Claude independent): LT2601 -> 100 questions/23 groups, RT2605 -> 100 questions/20 groups, both idempotent; LT+RT coexist in one DB (200 questions/43 groups/0 without answer). MILESTONE: full Listening+Reading TEXT pipeline (parse .docx/.doc + merge .xlsx keys + import to bank) works on real partner data. Remaining: compiled-MP3 audio rework (Listening audio model). Tasks A1 (fe11fa3) + A2 (c019be0) + A3 (d2388e8) + PARSE-006 (8239a88 + c65eb57) + PARSE-007 (a6fdf99) + PARSE-008 (37c25cc + cfd5431) + PARSE-009 (4668e44 + 0712a8a) + PARSE-010 (e2941f3).

features.name: TOEIC & B1 Exam Generator
features.description: Retrieves questions from the approved question bank and matches exam blueprints dynamically.
features.evidence: backend/app/services/toeic_generator.py — TOEIC generator near-complete (verified by test_toeic_generator.py + test_specs_generation.py & test_specs_bank.py): status='approved' filter (BANK-001), seeded reproducibility (GEN-005), fail-fast pre-checks (GEN-006), Part 7 subset-sum to exactly 200 questions (GEN-001), adaptive topic-diversity cap per part (GEN-003), and balanced correct-answer distribution A/B/C/D 20-28% via clone permutation (GEN-002) — all active. Known gaps tracked as xfail/planned: MATRIX-002 whole-exam difficulty ratio (entangled with unique P7 subset-sum solution), GEN-004 (cross-exam overlap, B6 — needs batch generation). Blueprint-as-Data implemented (GEN-007): core generate_exam(db, structure, ...) data-driven from a Blueprint structure (DB record or constant); generate_toeic_exam is a thin wrapper over TOEIC_BLUEPRINT — add VSTEP/HSK = new Blueprint record, no algorithm change. NOTE: verified on synthetic conftest fixtures; real partner data (separate .xlsx answer keys, legacy .doc, compiled MP3) needs the real Parser track.

features.name: Automated Exam Validator Gate
features.description: Checks generated exams against constraints, question overlap limits (<40%), and content completeness.
